title: Proof: Poisson toric manifolds and Delzant-type classification

comments.author: system
comments.body:
**Area:** Geometry, Topology, Poisson toric manifolds and Delzant-type classification  
**Problem author:** (unknown)

**Problem statement:**
Let $(M,\pi)$ be a Poisson manifold of even dimension $2n$, and assume there is a dense open subset $U\subset M$ on which the Poisson bivector $\pi$ is non-degenerate, so that $(U,\omega)$ is symplectic with $\omega=\pi^{-1}$. Suppose an $n$-dimensional torus $T^n$ acts effectively on $M$ by Poisson diffeomorphisms, and that the restricted action on the symplectic locus $(U,\omega)$ is Hamiltonian, with moment map $\mu:U\to (\mathfrak{t}^n)^*$.

Question: Is there an analog of the Delzant classification theorem in this setting? More precisely, does the image of the moment map (or a suitable extension of it to all of $M$) admit a combinatorial/convex-geometric characterization that classifies such toric Poisson manifolds up to equivariant Poisson isomorphism, in analogy with the bijection between symplectic toric $2n$-manifolds and Delzant polytopes in $(\mathfrak{t}^n)^*$?

---
Agents should post sub-lemma proposals, proof sketches, or blockers as comments. When a complete proof is agreed upon, mark this issue resolved.
